title: Architecture Decomposition
description: High-level architecture decomposition produced by /plan. Describes components, their interfaces, dependencies, wiring, and composition root.

spec_number.description: Spec number from the spec file frontmatter (e.g., 'SPEC-016'). Propagated through the pipeline so code-agent can tag created types with solid-spec frontmatter.

mode.description: Pipeline mode from spec frontmatter. 'rewrite' causes validate-plan to skip search and classify all components as create. Defaults to 'default' if absent.

spec_summary.description: One-line summary of what's being built

components.description: All types in the architecture — services, ViewModels, views, data models, protocols. Data models use category 'model' and populate fields[].

name.description: Type name following create-type naming conventions

category.description: solid-category vocabulary value (e.g., network, viewmodel, model, repository)

stack.description: solid-stack vocabulary values (e.g., swiftui, structured-concurrency). Empty array if pure language code.

responsibility.description: One sentence describing what this component does

interfaces.description: Protocols this component exposes. Empty array for data models.

dependencies.description: Protocols this component consumes (injected). Empty array for data models.

produces.description: Data types this component outputs. Empty array for data models.

fields.description: Field declarations (e.g., 'id: String', 'name: String'). Populated for data models (category: 'model'), empty array for other types.

composition_root.description: Name of the factory or assembly point where concrete types are wired together

acceptance_criteria.description: Verbatim acceptance criteria from the spec's user stories. Carried through the pipeline so downstream phases can verify fulfillment.

story.description: The user story this criterion belongs to (e.g., 'As a user, I want to filter products')

criteria.description: Individual acceptance criteria — verbatim from spec, not summarized

design_references.description: Design assets and mockups from the spec. Includes inline markdown (ASCII mockups, Mermaid diagrams) and file paths to resources/.

type.description: inline: content is the markdown/ASCII directly. file: content is a path to a resource file.

content.description: The design content (inline markdown) or file path (relative to spec)

label.description: Short description of what this design shows (e.g., 'Main screen layout', 'Data flow diagram')

technical_requirements.description: Verbatim technical requirements from the spec — type definitions, package structure, usage patterns, API signatures. Carried through the pipeline so the code agent has exact specifications.

section.description: Subsection heading from Technical Requirements (e.g., 'Package Structure', 'Type Definitions', 'Consumer Usage Pattern')

content.description: Verbatim markdown content including code blocks — not summarized

test_plan.description: Test cases extracted from Test Plan section. Each entry is a specific test to implement, classified by type and associated with a component where possible.

description.description: Verbatim test case description from the spec

given.description: Preconditions — what state is set up before the action (e.g., 'WindowManager has one tracked project')

when.description: Action — what is performed (e.g., 'open the same project URL again')

expect.description: Expected outcome — what should happen (e.g., 'existing window is brought to front, no new window created')

component.description: Name of the component this test targets. Null if the test spans multiple components or can't be associated with one.
